authentication.instructions: Authenticate every request with your API key in the 'X-Api-Key' header. The key is scoped to a single partner and grants access to all of that partner's stores.

rateLimiting.description: Requests are rate limited per API key to 120 requests per 1-minute window. When you exceed the limit the API responds with HTTP 429 Too Many Requests, including a 'Retry-After' header (integer number of seconds to wait before retrying) plus 'RateLimit-Limit', 'RateLimit-Remaining' and 'RateLimit-Reset' (seconds until the window resets). Respect 'Retry-After' before sending further requests.

endpoints.summary: Returns a machine-readable catalog of every available API endpoint, including each request's full URL, path/query parameters, request-body shape and a ready-to-use example — for programmatic discovery by the partner's integration code or AI agent.
Returns the partner's stores, each with its identifier and active domain.
Uploads the store's favicon from a base64-encoded image (raw base64 or a data URI) and returns the stored URL.
Uploads the store's logo from a base64-encoded image (raw base64 or a data URI) and returns the stored URL.
Sets the bank account (26-digit NRB) that cash-on-delivery payments are settled to for a delivery broker. Send an empty value to clear it.
Returns the carrier services offered by a delivery broker attached to the store. Use a service identifier from this list when adding a delivery method.
Returns the delivery methods currently configured for the store, with pricing per price group.
Adds a delivery method to the store. Requires a delivery broker already attached to the store and a price group (see the options endpoint).
Removes a delivery method from the store.
Updates an existing delivery method — its name, price, price group, visibility, delivery mode and cash-on-delivery flag.

Returns the delivery brokers attached to the store (including whether each supports cash on delivery and already has a settlement bank account) and the store's price groups — the identifiers required to add a delivery method.
Sets the order in which the store's delivery methods are shown at checkout.
Returns all custom domains configured for the store, each with its DNS records and verification status.
Adds a custom domain to the store together with its www variant, and returns the DNS records that must be created at the domain's registrar.
Activates a custom domain, promoting it to serve the store's storefront.
Returns the integrations available to the store (analytics, pixels, tag manager, Hotjar, SMS sender, live chat) with their active state and full configuration, including any configured API key.

Updates an integration's configuration. Send only the fields that apply to the integration's type — for example pixelId (and apiKey where the platform supports it) for pixels, containerId for Google Tag Manager, hotjarId for Hotjar, senderId for SMS, liveChatId for live chat.
Enables or disables an integration for the store. The first call enables it; each subsequent call flips its state.
Returns a paginated list of the media library shared by the store's organisation. Use a media id when setting a product image or a variant image.

Uploads one or more files (Base64 encoded, plain or as a data URI) to the media library and returns the created media with its id and public URL. Upload an image here first, then reference the returned id as a product image or a variant image.
Removes a file from the media library.

Returns a paginated list of the store's orders, sorted by order date descending, each with its product lines and payments (type, status, amount, provider). Contains no customer personal data. Filter by date range using the 'from' and 'to' query parameters.

Returns a single order with its product lines and all payments recorded against it — payment type, status, amount, whether it was cash on delivery or BLIK, and which provider the payment came from. Contains no customer personal data.

Returns where the order came from — the visitor sessions behind it (traffic source, referrer, entry page, device, page views, duration), the buyer's purchase path, any advertising click ids, and a summary of the attributed source. Returns 404 when the order has no visitor session attached.
Returns the store's pages, each with its path and full public URL.
Creates a new subpage in the store.
Sets a page's custom-HTML mode and stores its raw HTML body. When isHtml is true the storefront serves this HTML instead of the page's sections.
Returns a paginated list of the store's products, each with its variants, prices, and checkout links.
Creates a new product in the store.
Returns the product's image gallery in display order, including which image is the default one.

Sets the product's image gallery — send the full list of media ids with their order and which one is the default. Media missing from the list is detached from the product. Upload files first through the media endpoint to obtain their ids.
Detaches a single image from the product gallery.
Turns multiple variants on or off for a product. Turning it off collapses the product back to a single variant.

Returns every variant of a product with its full configuration — prices per price group (sale, regular, discount rate, promotion flag), EAN, VAT rate, dimensions, weight, image and variant parameters.

Replaces the product's whole variant set in one call — send every variant you want the product to keep. Variants with an id are updated, variants without an id are created, and any existing variant missing from the list is deleted. Use the single-variant price endpoint for simple price changes. EAN and VAT are managed by the variant details endpoint.

priceGroups.currency: string (enum: PLN | GBP | EUR | USD | CZK | RUB | MXN | HUF)

endpoints.summary: Updates the checkout link (checkout slug) for a specific product variant.
Updates a variant's EAN, VAT rate, dimensions and weight. VAT is a fraction, for example 0.23 for 23%.

Changes the price of a single variant in one price group, leaving every other variant and price untouched. Omit priceGroupId to use the store's default price group. Omitted regularPrice, discountRate and isPromotionActive keep their current values.
Returns the store's price groups. Prices are always set per price group, so use these identifiers when changing variant prices.
Returns the variant parameter names (for example Size, Colour) and their values defined for the store's organisation. Use these identifiers when assigning parameters to variants.
Creates a new variant parameter name, for example Size or Colour.
Creates a new value for a variant parameter name, for example XL for Size.

guides.summary: Any page of a store can be switched into custom-HTML mode. In that mode the storefront serves a raw HTML document you provide instead of the page's visual sections, so you can build fully bespoke landing pages while keeping the store's domain, checkout and tracking.
notes: Create the page first with POST /stores/{websiteId}/pages, then set its HTML with PUT /stores/{websiteId}/pages/{locationId}/html.

Send isHtml=true to make the storefront serve your HTML, and put the full document in the html field. Setting isHtml=false returns the page to its normal section-based rendering while keeping the stored HTML.
The HTML is served for the exact page path; the home page is the page whose path is empty.
Provide a complete, self-contained HTML body. Inline your own styles and scripts — the storefront does not inject a theme layout around custom-HTML pages.

analytics.description: Custom-HTML pages run the store's client analytics SDK as the global window.trevio object. PageView and a periodic heartbeat fire automatically on every custom-HTML page — never call those yourself. Call the interaction methods below to record shopper behavior; each maps to one analytics event type. Conventions: every method takes a single options object; prices and totals are plain numbers in the store's currency; currency is an ISO code such as 'PLN'; an item is { productId, name, price, quantity }; identityType is one of EmailHash, PhoneHash, CustomerId, ExternalCustomerId. A generic window.trevio.track(eventType, fields) is also available for advanced use.

methods.example: window.trevio.viewItemList({ currency: 'PLN', items: [{ productId: 'SKU-1', name: 'Koszulka', price: 49.99 }] })
window.trevio.addToCart({ currency: 'PLN', productId: 'SKU-123', name: 'Koszulka', price: 49.99, quantity: 1 })
window.trevio.removeFromCart({ currency: 'PLN', productId: 'SKU-123', name: 'Koszulka', price: 49.99, quantity: 1 })

methods.signature: { currency, total, items: [{ productId, name, price, quantity }] }
methods.example: window.trevio.viewCart({ currency: 'PLN', total: 99.98, items: [{ productId: 'SKU-1', name: 'Koszulka', price: 49.99, quantity: 2 }] })

methods.signature: { currency, total, items: [{ productId, name, price, quantity }] }
methods.example: window.trevio.beginCheckout({ currency: 'PLN', total: 99.98, items: [{ productId: 'SKU-1', name: 'Koszulka', price: 49.99, quantity: 2 }] })
window.trevio.addShippingInfo({ currency: 'PLN', total: 99.98, deliveryMethod: 'Kurier', deliveryCost: 15, items: [{ productId: 'SKU-1', name: 'Koszulka', price: 49.99, quantity: 2 }] })
window.trevio.addPaymentInfo({ currency: 'PLN', total: 99.98, paymentType: 'card', items: [{ productId: 'SKU-1', name: 'Koszulka', price: 49.99, quantity: 2 }] })

methods.signature: { currency, orderId, orderNumber, total, items: [{ productId, name, price, quantity }], isCashOnDelivery }
methods.example: window.trevio.purchase({ currency: 'PLN', orderId: '1001', orderNumber: 'ORD-1001', total: 99.98, items: [{ productId: 'SKU-1', name: 'Koszulka', price: 49.99, quantity: 2 }], isCashOnDelivery: false })

methods.signature: { currency, orderId, orderNumber, total, items: [{ productId, name, price, quantity }] }
methods.example: window.trevio.purchaseOnDelivery({ currency: 'PLN', orderId: '1001', orderNumber: 'ORD-1001', total: 99.98, items: [{ productId: 'SKU-1', name: 'Koszulka', price: 49.99, quantity: 2 }] })

publicStorefront.description: Public storefront endpoints used to run the shop — the same API the storefront frontend calls (product catalog, cart, checkout, orders, payments, delivery methods, discount codes and store configuration). No API key is required. These endpoints are served directly by the store API host shown in each request URL and are not routed through the partner API gateway. Identify the store by its domain or website id as shown in each request.
publicStorefront.authentication: None — these endpoints are public and do not require an API key.

endpoints.summary: Returns the public storefront configuration for a domain.
Returns all initial storefront data for a domain — configuration, layout, theme, content locations, scripts, settings, categories and add-ons — in a single request.
Returns the current shopping cart for a client on a given store.

queryParameters.name: languageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Saves the customer's checkout details for the cart, including contact data, delivery address, invoice details and the selected delivery method or pickup point.
Removes the applied discount code from the cart.
Applies a discount code to the cart and returns the recalculated totals.
Returns the identifiers of the products a client has saved as favorites on a given store.
Adds a product to the client's saved favorites for a store.
Removes a product from the client's saved favorites.
Adds a product variant to the shopping cart.
Removes a product variant from the shopping cart.
Updates the quantity of a product variant already in the shopping cart.
Returns the hierarchical tree of product categories for a store's public storefront.

queryParameters.name: LanguageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Submits a contact form message from a storefront visitor and delivers it to the store's inbox.
Returns the delivery methods available to a shopper in a store, including their pricing tiers and free-shipping thresholds.
Returns the shipping-broker configuration needed to render the pickup-point selector for a given delivery method.
Retrieves the details of a discount code by its identifier for a given store.
Returns the content of a specified legal or informational document for a store, such as terms of service or a privacy policy.
Returns the paginated list of e-books available in a store's public catalog.

queryParameters.name: LanguageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Returns the public details of a single e-book identified by its catalog number.

queryParameters.name: LanguageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Returns the current resolved price for a specific e-book product in a store.
Looks up a Polish company's registration details in the national business registry by its tax identification number.
Returns the custom HTML content configured for a specific path on a storefront domain.
Returns the third-party integration and tracking scripts configured for a storefront domain.
Returns the page layout and section structure of a storefront for a domain.
Returns the configured custom content placement locations for a storefront domain.
Creates a new return request for selected order items on a store.
Returns the current status and details of a specific return request.
Downloads the return shipping label file for a return request.
Looks up an order by its number and customer email to preview which items are eligible for return, the return window, and any existing return requests.
Returns the available return shipping methods and their prices for an order's parcel.
Returns the details of a specific order placed by a customer on a given store.

queryParameters.name: languageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Returns the status and activity history timeline for a specific order.
Downloads the PDF invoice document for a specific order.
Places a new order from the current contents of the shopping cart at checkout.

requestBody.languageCode: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Creates an order for a one-time purchase of a digital product.

requestBody.languageCode: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Creates a recurring subscription order for a crowdfunding-backed digital subscription tier.

requestBody.languageCode: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Creates a recurring subscription order for a standard digital subscription tier.
Lists the digital download items a customer is entitled to for a given order.
Generates a secure, time-limited token for downloading a purchased digital product on an order.
Returns the enabled payment methods available at checkout for a given store.
Starts processing an existing payment using the selected payment provider, optionally with a BLIK code.
Returns the current processing status of a payment by its identifier.
Resolves a payment from its public identifier within a given store, returning its details.
Reports a broken storefront page or category encountered by a shopper so the issue can be logged for the store.
Returns a paginated list of products from a store's public catalog, with optional filtering and sorting.

queryParameters.name: LanguageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

queryParameters.name: Order
queryParameters.type: string (enum: Newest | Discount | Popular | PriceAsc | PriceDesc)

endpoints.summary: Returns the delivery methods available for a product.
Returns the rich content description sections for a product.
Returns the published customer reviews for a product.
Submits a customer review for a product, including any uploaded photo attachments.
Returns the full public details of a single product identified by its catalog number.

queryParameters.name: LanguageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Resolves a direct-checkout slug into its corresponding purchasable product and variant for a store.
Generates an XML product feed for a store in the Ceneo price-comparison format.

queryParameters.name: languageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Exports a store's product catalog as a downloadable CSV file.

queryParameters.name: languageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Generates an XML product feed for a store in the Facebook catalog format.

queryParameters.name: languageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Generates an XML product feed for a store in the Google Shopping catalog format.

queryParameters.name: languageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Exports a store's product catalog as a JSON document.

queryParameters.name: languageCode
queryParameters.type: string (enum: PL | EN | DE | FR | ES | IT | CS | SK | UK)

endpoints.summary: Returns the general public settings for a storefront domain.
Returns the visual theme, including colors and typography, for a storefront domain.
